mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction during periods of rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX forward returns are not significantly higher when both series expand concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns during early-cycle expansions, or the 5-day directional accuracy falls below 50% over a 1-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a 0.25% monthly growth threshold, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 60% of high-inflation trigger months, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer confidence and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields a negative Rank IC over a 3-year rolling window, or SPX returns are not significantly higher when both labor indicators improve simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue fundamentals, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward SPX returns over a 5-year sample, or directional accuracy drops below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and increase in consumer prices signals stagflationary pressures that compress profit margins and trigger risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month while CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in > 55% of stagflation trigger instances, or the joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during supply-demand mismatches.
